data.title: Nvidia Stock Rises. Why More Volatility Is Coming. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Nvidia stock was rising early Wednesday as it continued to pare back some of its recent losses — but more big swings could be ahead.

Shares in the chip maker rose 1.4% to $110.31 in premarket trading, having closed the previous session up 1.7%. Futures tracking the benchmark S&P 500 index climbed 0.6%.

Nvidia is down 17% over the past month through Tuesday's close, tumbling as investors have fretted that U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff plans will drag down growth and trigger a flare-up of inflation, both of which could weigh on chip sales. The White House would likely have to scale back its trade policies for the wild swings to end.

Another factor that could impact shares is the Trump administration's push to boost semiconductor manufacturing in the U.S. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. has pitched Nvidia and rival chip designers Advanced Micro Devices and Broadcom the idea of taking stakes in a joint venture to operate Intel's factories, Reuters reported early Wednesday, citing four sources familiar with the matter.

TSMC, AMD, Broadcom, Intel, and Nvidia didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from Barron's.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Wednesday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
EU Hits Back as U.S. Steel and Aluminum Tariffs Take Effect

The 25% global levy was imposed by the Trump administration after the U.S. and Canada backed away from additional measures. The European Union responded with retaliatory tariffs.

Eurozone Economy Still Burdened by High Rates, ECB's Centeno Warns

Rate setter Mario Centeno said the European Central Bank should continue to cut interest rates or risk seeing inflation settle beneath its target.

Trump's Economic Messaging Is Spooking Some of His Own Advisers

The president's team has received a flood of calls from business executives concerned about the mixed messaging on tariffs.

Recession Worries Seize the Market. How to Really Know What's Coming.

While the market fixates on tariffs, economists examine consumer spending, business investment and labor markets to determine the likelihood of recession.

BOJ Reiterates That Bond Yields Should Be Determined in Market

Bank of Japan Gov. Kazuo Ueda reiterated that bond yields should be determined freely in the market, suggesting that the central bank doesn't have any immediate plans to intervene to stop yield rises.

The anywhere-but-America trade has been working. But there are limits.

Investors have been rewarded for investing outside the United States - but real headwinds are emerging that could limit the successful trade.

Why a 'good' CPI report on Wednesday may not be enough to turn stock-market carnage around

Wednesday's consumer-price index report is now in sharp focus with investors hoping more clarity on stagflation concerns could help stem the stock-market selloff.

Goldman Sachs Cuts Forecast for U.S. Economy. Trump's Tariffs Are to Blame.

Chief Economist Jan Hatzius also anticipates core PCE inflation may reaccelerate to 3% later this year.

The Stock Market's Decline Offers Two Silver Linings

A lower 10-year yield, and more corporate stock buybacks will bring back more buyers to the market.

House Passes GOP Measure to Avert Government Shutdown

The vote sets up a showdown in the Senate over a bill Democrats criticize as giving more power to President Trump and Elon Musk.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Wednesday at 5 AM ET
data.description:
Asahi Looks to Invest More in the U.S. as President Trump Wields Tariffs

The Japanese drink maker is investing more in the U.S., paying heed to President Trump's tariff threats, which has led many global companies to recalibrate their investment strategies.

China Summons Walmart for Talks as Suppliers Complain Over Tariffs

Beijing has summoned Walmart for reportedly asking Chinese suppliers to swallow the tariff hikes imposed by the Trump administration, marking the latest salvo in the trade spat between the world's two largest economies.

Porsche Plans 3,900 Job Cuts in Efficiency Drive

The company warned investments and costs of reshaping will hit earnings this year and expects markets to remain challenging.

Rheinmetall Sales Soar to Record $10.65 Billion, But Miss Target

While 2024 sales grew 35%, boosted by increased demand, there was a dip in sales at its power systems and trade divisions at the start of of the year.

Cathay Pacific Annual Profit Rises Slightly but Flags Trade Risks

The airline said its annual net profit rose slightly but flagged that trade conflicts could pose risks to its cargo business.

Inditex Expansion Plan on Track Amid Fierce Competition in Fast Fashion

The owner of fashion brand Zara said store and online sales rose 4% at constant currency compared with the prior-year period.

Roche to Develop Weight-Loss Therapy With Zealand Pharma

The companies agreed to share profits for the therapy equally in the U.S. and Europe, but Roche will have exclusive rights to commercialization in the rest of the world.

Kohl's Shares Fall After Weak Sales Outlook

The department-store chain joins a growing list of companies warning of a slowdown in household spending.

Oracle won some big cloud contracts. Here's why its stock is falling.

Oracle Corp. regaled Wall Street on Monday with talk about the future, enormous demand and some very large cloud-services contracts, but its stock continued a downward trend over concerns about growing costs associated with building out more data centers.

Nvidia Stock Sees Wild Swings. How Trump Could End the AI Chips Slump.

The company's shares dropped 5.1% Monday for their lowest close since September.

data.title: M.O.B.A. Network: Positive Outlook Highlighted in Recent Redeye Analysis
data.description:
Stockholm, March 12, 2025 - M.O.B.A. Network ("M.O.B.A.") is pleased to highlight the recent coverage published by Redeye on March 11, 2025, titled "M.O.B.A. Network: Rebound in Sight". The analysis presents a positive outlook for M.O.B.A. Network, noting that the company appears to have reached a turning point following four consecutive quarters of growth and improved margins.

Redeye emphasizes M.O.B.A. Network's strengthened platform and its improved monetization potential, particularly as gaming and advertising markets show signs of recovery. The report outlines a fair value range of SEK 5–20 with a base case valuation of SEK 11, indicating significant upside potential from the current share price.

Additionally, the report highlights M.O.B.A.'s unique position in the gaming community space, its potential to scale subscription revenues, and the attractive opportunities presented by a shift toward first-party data as identifiers are phasing out.

Please note that the report reflects Redeye's independent assessment, analysis, and forecast. The conclusions drawn, as well as the valuation range presented, are solely the views of Redeye and do not necessarily represent the opinions or guidance of M.O.B.A. Network's management. M.O.B.A. Network assumes no responsibility for the correctness of the report's information or projections.

data.title: REG-ROCKWOOL A/S – transactions in connection with share buy-back programme
data.description:
Company announcement

for ROCKWOOL A/S

Release no. 14 – 2025

to Nasdaq Copenhagen        

12 March 2025

ROCKWOOL A/S – transactions in connection with share buy-back programme

As mentioned in announcement no. 07/2025, ROCKWOOL A/S has initiated a share buy-back programme which will run from 7 February 2025 until 5 February 2026. During this period, the Company will buy own shares for up to a maximum of 150 MEUR.

The programme is implemented in accordance with EU Commission Regulation No 596/2014 of 16 April 2014 and EU Commission Delegated Regulation No 2016/1052 of 8 March 2016, which together constitute the “Safe Harbour” regulation.

The following transactions have been executed during the period 5 – 11 March 2025:

DateNumber of B sharesAverage purchase priceB shares (DKK)Aggregate amount, B shares (DKK)
[Accumulated, last announcement]32,800 90,949,022
5 March 20255002,961.921,480,960
6 March 20257503,189.442,392,080
7 March 20255003,136.991,568,495
10 March 20258003,103.042,482,432
11 March 20258003,114.942,491,952
Accumulated under the programme (B shares)36,150 101,364,941

With the transactions stated above, ROCKWOOL A/S owns 553,363 B shares corresponding to 2.56 percent of the Company’s total share capital.

An overview showing the transaction data for the period 5 – 11 March 2025 is enclosed.

Further information:        

Kim Junge Andersen

Senior Vice President, CFO

ROCKWOOL A/S

+45 46 55 80 15

Attachments

  • SE-2025-14_EN
  • SE-2025-14_Transactions B shares

data.title: Major Japanese Firms Promise Big Pay Raises in Bright Sign for Economy
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--Major Japanese companies have promised large pay increases for their workers this year, indicating that Japan's economy is making progress toward the wage-led growth policymakers want to achieve after decades of stagnation.

On Wednesday, large companies including auto giant Toyota Motor concluded the annual spring wage talks known as "shunto," with many saying they will fully meet unions' salary demands.

Sustained inflation and solid corporate profits are supporting wage hike momentum in Japan, an important piece of the virtuous cycle policymakers are seeking to create, in which higher income promotes spending and creates mild inflation. More evidence of strengthening wages will likely firm up expectations that the Bank of Japan will keep raising interest rates.

Toyota met its union's request for pay rises in full for a fifth straight year. The union has asked for an increase in monthly wages of between 9,950 yen and 24,450 yen, equivalent to about $67.33-$165.45, depending on job type and seniority.

Electronics conglomerate Hitachi also agreed to a record 6.2% average raise in monthly wages.

"This is a particularly important year in terms of establishing the momentum for wage increases as the Japanese economy aims to completely escape from deflation," said Susumu Takimoto, an executive in charge of human resources at Hitachi.

Ohsho Food Service, which operates a chain of Chinese restaurants, offered a larger-than-requested increase of 8.2% on average.

The moves come as the country's largest labor union group is scheduled to release preliminary results for its wage talks on Friday that will set the tone for discussions at many of Japan's smaller companies, which employ the bulk of the workforce.

The Japanese Trade Union Confederation, known as Rengo, said last week that its member unions are seeking an average wage increase of 6.09%--the biggest request in three decades. In 2024, large companies gave a 5.1% raise to workers, helping prompt the BOJ to unwind its long-standing monetary easing program.

If the 2025 shunto results are as strong as expected, that would back the case for the BOJ to consider an additional rate hike in the coming months, said Mizuho Securities economist Ryosuke Katagi.

But since it is likely well-anticipated by the BOJ that this year's outcome will be as strong as last year's, that "may not encourage the bank to speed up the pace of rate increases," Katagi said.

Both the government and the central bank are hoping that higher household incomes will help fuel a recovery in private consumption. Recent government data showed that household spending rose just 0.8% in January from a year earlier as consumers reduced purchases of food due to the higher costs.

"A rise in consumer spending remains moderate relative to wage growth," Japan's economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said on Wednesday.

"Japan is currently at a turning point that determines whether it can shift towards economic growth driven by wage increases and investments," he said.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Bulgaria Trade Deficit Widens in January
data.description:
Bulgaria’s trade deficit widened to BGN 1,974.5 million in January 2025 from BGN 1,044.3 million in the corresponding month of the previous year.

Imports surged by 10.4% year-on-year to BGN 8,187.6 million, primarily attributed to higher purchases from non-EU countries (30.8%).

However, purchases declined from EU countries (-2.6%).

Meanwhile, exports fell by 2.5% from a year earlier to BGN 6,213.1 million, mainly due to a notable decrease of sales to non-EU countries (-10.5%).

Conversely, sales to EU countries (1.9%) increased.

data.title: Malaysia's Employment Rises 0.3% in January as Services Sector Gains
data.description:
Malaysia's employment rose 0.3% in January, adding 54,500 jobs to reach 16.7 million, government data showed on Tuesday.

The employment-to-population ratio increased to 68.5% from 68.4% in December, driven by services, manufacturing, and construction gains.

The services sector led the increase, with growth in accommodation and food services, wholesale and retail trade, and healthcare.

Employee numbers rose 0.3% to 12.6 million, while self-employed workers increased 0.7% to 3.1 million.

The unemployment rate held at 3.1%, with the unemployed falling 0.9% to 533,800. The labor force grew 0.3% to 17.22 million, keeping the participation rate steady at 70.6%.

The data comes as Malaysia moves to bolster its job market.

Nomura appointed Ong Kin Yok as Managing Director for Malaysia and Singapore to expand wealth management.

Meanwhile, Petronas plans to "rightsize" its workforce for long-term sustainability, while a Malaysia-Singapore special economic zone in Johor is expected to create 20,000 jobs.

However, concerns remain over Johor's growing data center market, which is driving up resource demand without significant job creation.

Malaysia also signed a $250 million deal with Arm Holdings for chip design and engineer training, aiming for up to $2 billion in annual revenue from local chip firms.

data.title: Oman & Emirates Investment to Halt Trading in Abu Dhabi
data.description:
Oman & Emirates Investment Holding Co. (ADX:OEIHC) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi bourse from 1:30 pm local time on Wednesday due to its board meeting.

The investment manager will resume trading after the meeting results are disclosed, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Leonardo's Rating Cut to Hold From Buy By Equita — Market Talk
data.description: Leonardo's rating was cut to hold from buy by Equita, after the Italian aerospace and defense group failed to provide a much-expected update on the future of its loss-making aerostructures division at a call with investors Tuesday. Equita's analyst Martino De Ambroggi said any solution to anticipate break-even would boost shares. He also noted that Leonardo's ambition to make acquisitions in the cyber-space domain "could become expensive given the re-rating of the sector multiples." The company has set aside up to 500 million euros for acquisitions this year. "There is no doubt that Europe is at the beginning of an unprecedented cycle of military investments, but the effects will be diluted over time (timing to be clarified), revisions cannot be ruled out and several countries had already planned an increase," he added. Shares are down 4% at 41.96 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.description:

data.title: Kingswood Shares Fall on Discounted $62 Million Takeover Bid
data.description:
By Anthony O. Goriainoff

Shares in Kingswood fell to a six-year low after the wealth manager said it was in advanced talks with HSQ Investments about a possible discounted 48 million pounds ($62.2 million) takeover bid.

In morning trading, London-listed shares were down 15% at 7.25 pence and are down 31% over the past 12 months.

The wealth and investment management company said the possible cash offer for the capital not already owned by HSQ was 7 pence for each Kingswood share, an 18% discount to Tuesday's closing price of 8.50 pence.

Kingswood said its performance had been hurt by headwinds affecting the sector in the last couple of years, and that it hadn't grown as strongly as management expected.

The company said its imminent cash obligations significantly exceeded its available cash balance at the end of March, and that in the absence of external support it wouldn't be able to satisfy these obligations.

Since the start of 2024, HSQ, which holds 68.4% of Kingswood's voting rights, has provided the firm with loans amounting to 21 million pounds to satisfy its working capital obligations, Kingswood said.

Kingswood said HSQ confirmed that it continued to be supportive of the business but that it would only be willing to provide short-term funding if it were sole institutional shareholder, with Kingswood as an unlisted, private company.

The firm said there wasn't any certainty an offer would be made and that further announcements would be made when appropriate.

Write to Anthony O. Goriainoff at anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com

data.title: Crude Oil Prices Gain on Weaker U.S. Dollar, but Remain Under Pressure
data.description:
Crude oil prices rose early on Wednesday on a weaker US dollar, but remained near recent lows amid concerns about an economic slowdown in the U.S. and tariff impacts globally.

Brent crude gained 0.6% to US$69.96/barrel and West Texas Intermediate crude rose 0.7% to US$66.69/b at last look. Oil holding steady in a positive position signals that near-term crude demand remains strong, Reuters reported, citing analysts at ANZ.

"Easing dollar counters the bearish bias of global economic slowdown, although this seems short-lived," Reuters quoted Priyanka Sachdeva, senior market analyst at Phillip Nova, as saying.

The U.S. Dollar Index traded 0.4% lower early on Wednesday at 103.47.

U.S. stock prices dropped again on Tuesday amid the biggest selloff in months amid tariff worries and souring consumer sentiment.

Meanwhile, U.S. crude oil production is expected to set a larger record this year than previously estimated at 13.61 million barrels per day on average, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in its latest Short-Term Energy Outlook.

data.title: UK's Legal & General falls on annual operating profit miss
data.description:
** Legal & General falls 1.5% to 241.2p; among top pct loser on FTSE 100 index CURRENCYCOM:UK100

** British life insurer reports FY total operating profit of 1.71 bln pounds ($2.21 bln) vs year-ago 1.67 bln pounds, below Jefferies' estimate of 1.75 bln pounds

** Brokerage says earnings appear to be broadly as consensus-expected, with only low single-digit variances across board

** LGEN confirms plan to return more than 5 bln pounds to investors via buybacks and dividends over the next three years

** Stock up 6.57% so far this year, as of last close

($1 = 0.7737 pounds)

data.title: German spot down, French up on mixed weather signals
data.description:
European prompt power prices showed different directions on Wednesday, with Germany's falling on higher wind generation and France's rising along with more demand triggered by colder weather.

German day-ahead (TRDEBD1) power fell by 2.8% to 122.8 euros ($134.10) per megawatt hour at 0900 GMT.

French baseload for the day-ahead (TRFRBD1) was up 6.2% at 116.3 euros/MWh.

Germany was expecting wind output to rise to 5.9 gigawatts (GW) on Thursday, up 600 megawatts (MW), while French wind output was expected to rise by 300 MW to reach 3.1 GW, LSEG data showed.

French nuclear availability was unchanged at 77% of total capacity.

Power consumption in Germany was seen edging up by 300 MW to reach 59.5 GW, and by 2.4 GW in France to 58 GW in the day-on-day period.

German year-ahead baseload (TRDEBYZ6) was 0.6% down at 82.5 euros/MWh and the French equivalent position (TRFRBYZ6) untraded after a close at 63.9 euros.

The European carbon market's benchmark 2025 contract (CFI2Zc1) was little changed at 68.0 euros per metric ton.

Gas markets in the remainder of the month are expected to soften, in response to strong European LNG imports driven by lower demand in Asia and high U.S. LNG supplies, said a report from Swiss utility Axpo, tying this to predictions of declining prices in the wider energy market.

"The influence of policy is currently very intense and presents serious challenges for energy producers, traders and consumers alike," it also noted, referring to tariff wars.

German statistics office Destatis said that in 2024, 431.5 billion kilowatt hours (kWh) of electricity were generated in the country and fed into the grid, 3.6% less than in 2023.

German utility RWE said it has agreed to supply French oil major TotalEnergies with about 30,000 tonnes of green hydrogen a year from 2030, in what would be the biggest amount ever contracted from a German electrolysis facility.

($1 = 0.9158 euros)

data.title: Regarding to exercise the call options
data.description:
Refer to the previous announcement made today, below is the correctannouncement.

We refer to the announcement published 3 March 2025 regarding the exercise ofPembroke Olive Downs Pty Ltd's call option to redeem all outstanding bondsunderits senior secured bond issue with ISIN NO0011173536. The exercise of the call option was subject to certain conditions as set outinthe call option notice being satisfied or waived 3 business days before thecalloption repayment date of 17 March 2025. We are pleased confirm that the conditions for the call option have beensatisfied and that all outstanding bonds will be redeemed as set out in theannouncement published on 3 March 2025. The entire bond issue will be repaidata price equal to 105.00 per cent. of the Nominal Amount for each redeemed bond(plus accrued and unpaid interest on the redeemed amount), with record date on13 March 2025 and settlement date 17 March 2025.

data.title: Italian Stocks Rebound
data.description:
The FTSE MIB climbed more than 1% to 38,307 in early trading on Wednesday, rebounding after three straight declines and aligning with regional peers.

Market sentiment improved after the US agreed to resume military aid to Ukraine following Kyiv’s acceptance of a US-proposed 30-day ceasefire with Russia.

Meanwhile, the EU will impose €26bn (£22bn) in trade countermeasures on US goods in response to President Trump’s steel and aluminum tariffs.

On the corporate front, Banca Popolare di Sondrio plans to return €1.5 billion to shareholders by 2027 to fend off a takeover bid from BPER Banca.

Both stocks rose over 2%.

Other notable gainers included Prysmian (+4%), UniCredit (+2.4%), Pirelli (+2%), and Nexi (+2%).

On the downside, shares fell for ERG (-4%) and Leonardo (-2.9%).

data.title: UK's Hochschild Mining soars on restoring dividend payout
data.description:
** Hochschild soars ~13% to 216.5p; set for biggest intraday gain since July 2023

** Top gainer on London's mid caps index

** Precious metals mining co restores dividend payout; recommends final dividend of 1.94 cents

** Peel Hunt says co's dividend policy "looks to be a balance between investor returns, while sustaining the ability to invest in growth assets"

** Co posts 54% y/y growth in FY24 adjusted EBITDA

** HOCM drops 2.4% so far this year, as of last close

data.title: South African Rand Weakens Ahead of Budget
data.description:
The South African rand weakened slightly to around 18.3 per USD as traders grew nervous ahead of the budget speech later in the day.

Finance Minister was set to present a revised annual budget that was originally scheduled for delivery three weeks ago but was postponed at the last minute due to disagreements within the ruling coalition over a proposal to raise the value-added tax (VAT) by 2 percentage points to 17%.

The amended budget is expected to propose a smaller VAT increase, likely as a concession to help secure its approval.

Meanwhile, local media reports suggested that there was still no agreement between the two largest political parties in the coalition government (GNU).
